The campaign couldn’t get more NYC (specifically Lower East Side) with the Bowery-based vintage boutique behind the design and New York-native Liim fronting the debut.Shortly after sharing a preview depicting a plastic-wrapped plaid couch, the Liim photos landed, featuring the matching plaid uppers and brown

articles.title: From DJ Booths to Fairways, SOSUPERSAM Finds Her Flow as a Hidden Hypegolfer
articles.summary: Hidden Hypegolfers is a series built around the ones you wouldn’t immediately clock as golfers. However, upon closer look, it all clicks. They may be creatives, athletes, and cultural figures known for something else entirely. They can be an artist, a DJ or a stylist, but whatever lane they dominate, golf quietly lives in their routine. They’re not switching identities but just expanding them, all while bringing the same mindset, style, and discipline from their craft onto the course. Golf becomes another layer of their lifestyle and it's where performance, personal expression, and culture intersect. With Oakley also in focus, the brand highlights this crossover of sport and style as the connective thread across every facet of these creatives’ multi-hyphenate world.Samantha Duenas knows a

articles.title: JW ANDERSON Just Turned Diadora's 1975 Runner Into Your Next Obsession
articles.summary: Italian sportswear has quietly become one of fashion’s most enduring reference points. Long before luxury labels started mining the aesthetics of terraces, tracksuits and archival runners, brands like Diadora were already shaping the visual language of sport through performance design and technical craftsmanship. Now, that history is being pulled back into focus through a new collaboration with JW ANDERSON, marking the label’s first-ever project with the heritage footwear company.At the center of the release is the Equipe trainer, a silhouette originally introduced by Diadora in 1975 for elite runners. While plenty of archival sneakers get revived today, the Equipe carries a different kind of weight because it comes from a pre-sneaker-boom era when performance footwear was still deeply tie

Stead tannery in Leeds, both editions make the case that the 1461's enduring appeal lies less in reinvention and more in the precision of its construction.The material selection here carries real weight. articles.title: Aleali May’s New Clarks Wallabee Is a Corduroy Dream
articles.summary: Name: Aleali May x Clarks Originals WallabeeColorway: Grey Combi, Beige CombiSKU: 26185905, 26185907MSRP: €170 EURRelease Date: May 21Where to Buy: AFEWAleali May and Clarks Originals are teaming up for two collaborative Wallabee models, expanding the LA-based creative's collaborative footwear portfolio beyond the traditional sneaker realm. Shifting her focus to a certified staple of global streetwear, May brings her signature design language and elevated curation to the iconic silhouette, delivering a highly tactile update for the upcoming season.A granular breakdown of the new release reveals a rigorous focus on the shoe's physical construction. The upper is completely overhauled with a thick, ribbed corduroy fabric, introducing a rigid and dimensional feel to the classic moccasin shape.

articles.title: First Look at Action Bronson’s New Balance 1890 “Planet Frog”
articles.summary: Name: Action Bronson x New Balance 1890 “Planet Frog”SKU: TBCRelease Date: 2026Action Bronson has provided a first look at his latest collaboration with New Balance, revealing the 1890 in a “Planet Frog” colorway via a series of vibrant campaign images on his Instagram.True to its moniker, the silhouette continues Bronson’s playful approach to sneaker design, with an amphibious-inspired color palette dominated by varying shades of earth tones. The upper features a mix of green mesh and suede overlays, accented by earthy browns and pops of neon yellow that evoke amphibian-like hues. The quarters and tongue carry Bronson’s signature “BAKLAVA” branding, while the laces add contrast with a lighter shade that ties the palette together.
